participant.name: Dr Lea Martin
participant.persona_summary: Calm, articulate, pragmatic internist in Lyon

answers.Q4: Public hospital with one half-day per week in an outpatient clinic
answers.Q7: I usually check French society guidelines, UpToDate, and discuss tricky cases with colleagues during the week.
answers.Q8: I follow medical innovation through specialty newsletters, congress highlights, LinkedIn posts from physicians, and a couple of hospital innovation webinars each month.
answers.Q9: I mainly turn to a senior internist in my department and, for cardio-metabolic topics, a cardiology colleague I trust.
answers.Q10: I do not dislike pharma representatives, but I find many visits too promotional and not tailored enough to my real patients.
answers.Q11: Short evidence summaries, patient education materials I can reuse, and a digital follow-up with key trial data instead of long slide decks.
answers.Q12: Very limited during clinic hours; early lunch slots or brief end-of-day meetings work best.
answers.Q13: A focused 10-minute visit with one topic, transparent evidence, practical takeaways, and written follow-up I can review later.
answers.Q14: I collaborate fairly often with hospital and community pharmacists, especially for medication reconciliation and adherence questions after discharge.
answers.Q15: Pharmacists are useful for checking interactions, helping with adherence, and flagging practical issues patients have with their treatment.
answers.Q17: I would be open to more collaboration if it becomes more evidence-based, shorter, and integrated with digital tools rather than traditional sales visits.
answers.Q19: It should sound a bit less scripted at transitions, remember previous answers more explicitly, and avoid questions that feel too close together.
answers.Q20: Around 40 euros would feel fair for a 10 to 12 minute professional interview.
